meta_title: Impact Windows in Westgate, FL | Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
meta_description: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs hurricane-rated impact windows in Westgate, FL. Protect your home, lower insurance costs & meet Palm Beach County code. Free estimates!
h1: Impact Windows Built for Westgate's Storm Season — and Every Season After
intro:
Westgate, Florida sits squarely in the heart of Palm Beach County's residential corridor, and if you've lived here for more than a few years, you already know what South Florida storm season truly feels like. At roughly 7 miles from the Atlantic coastline, Westgate occupies a deceptively vulnerable position — far enough inland that some homeowners assume they're sheltered from the worst, yet close enough that every named storm rolling up the coast rattles windows, strips roofing, and leaves a trail of insurance claims through neighborhoods like the Westgate Community. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — is here to change that reality for good.

With an average home value of approximately $245,000 and more than half of the housing stock dating back to before 1994, Westgate is a community where investment protection matters deeply. Homes built before the sweeping Florida Building Code revisions that followed Hurricane Andrew were constructed to far lower wind-resistance standards — many featuring single-pane aluminum windows that offer almost no meaningful defense against hurricane-force winds, wind-driven debris, or even the persistent UV bombardment of South Florida summers. For families in these older homes, upgrading to certified impact-resistant windows isn't just a smart home improvement; it's one of the most financially sound decisions a Westgate homeowner can make. The combination of reduced insurance premiums, improved energy efficiency, and increased resale value routinely delivers a compelling return on investment.

Palm Beach County's wind zone designation for the 33406 ZIP code requires that all new windows and replacement windows meet a minimum 140 mph wind-speed rating — and while Westgate sits outside the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) that governs Miami-Dade and parts of Broward, the Florida Building Code still mandates the use of hurricane-rated products for any permitted window replacement in this area. That means your windows must carry the proper Notice of Acceptance (NOA) documentation and pass rigorous impact and pressure testing. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles every detail of this process, from product selection through permit filing with the Palm Beach County Building Division, so you never have to navigate the paperwork alone.

section_why.h2: Why Westgate Homeowners Choose Premier Impact Windows & Roofing
section_why.body:
When you invite a contractor into your home, you deserve more than a sales pitch — you deserve a team with the experience, credentials, and local knowledge to deliver a finished product you can trust for decades. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing brings more than 60 years of combined installation experience to every project in Westgate and across Palm Beach, Broward, and Miami-Dade counties. That depth of expertise means our technicians have seen every window configuration, every wall construction type, and every framing challenge that older Westgate homes can present — and they know exactly how to handle it.

One of the most important distinctions that sets Premier Impact Windows & Roofing apart in the South Florida market is our 100% in-house installation model. We do not subcontract your project. Every measurement, every installation, every final inspection is performed by our own trained and certified crews — not rotating third-party laborers who may never have set foot in a Palm Beach County home before. This matters enormously in a community like Westgate, where pre-1994 construction means older rough openings, non-standard frame depths, and aging stucco surrounds that require careful, experienced hands to handle without causing damage.

Our A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau and our track record of 500+ completed homes across South Florida didn't happen by accident. They reflect a company culture built around accountability, craftsmanship, and the kind of after-installation support that makes homeowners feel genuinely taken care of. We understand that for a Westgate family in the lower-middle income tier, a full window replacement represents a meaningful financial commitment — which is why we make the process as transparent and stress-free as possible, from the free in-home estimate through final permit sign-off.

We're also fully licensed and insured in the State of Florida, which protects your home and your wallet in the unlikely event that anything goes wrong during installation. When you're comparing contractors, don't just compare prices — compare accountability. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing delivers both competitive pricing and the peace of mind that comes from working with a contractor who stands behind every window we install.

section_history.h2: Westgate's Storm History Makes the Case for Impact Windows
section_history.body:
Westgate and the broader West Palm Beach corridor have faced at least seven named storms since 1990, and three in particular left lasting impressions on homeowners throughout Palm Beach County. Hurricane Frances made landfall in September 2004 as a Category 2 storm, causing widespread wind damage and flooding across Palm Beach County. Homes with older single-pane windows were especially vulnerable, as the sustained winds drove rain horizontally through failing seals and shattered glass — creating interior flooding that often caused more structural damage than the wind itself. Just over a year later, Hurricane Wilma arrived in October 2005 as the most intense Atlantic hurricane ever recorded at the time, delivering rapid-onset winds that caught many South Florida residents off guard. Wilma's damage in Palm Beach County was extensive, and Westgate's community of older homes bore significant losses.

More recently, Hurricane Irma tracked through South Florida in September 2017, and while it weakened before reaching the Palm Beach area, it still produced tropical storm to Category 1 conditions across the region. Irma served as a powerful reminder that even storms that don't make a direct hit can produce enough wind, debris, and rain infiltration to cause thousands of dollars in damage to homes without proper hurricane protection. For families in the Westgate Community who had postponed upgrading their windows, Irma was often the turning point.

The pattern across all of these storms is consistent: homes protected by impact-rated windows and doors experience dramatically less structural damage, less interior water intrusion, and faster recovery times than those relying on shutters, plywood, or original single-pane glass. Beyond the immediate storm damage, impact windows provide year-round protection against the wind-driven debris that South Florida's frequent afternoon thunderstorms can produce. Westgate may not sit on the beachfront, but seven major storm events in three decades make a compelling case for permanent, code-compliant hurricane protection.

section_code.h2: Florida Building Code Requirements for Impact Windows in Westgate and Palm Beach County
section_code.body:
Westgate falls within Palm Beach County's jurisdiction under the Florida Building Code (FBC), which establishes strict requirements for any window replacement or new installation in the region. The 33406 ZIP code is designated as a 140 mph wind zone, meaning all replacement windows must be engineered, tested, and certified to withstand a minimum of 140 mph sustained winds. This is not a guideline — it is a code requirement, and all permitted window work is inspected by the Palm Beach County Building Division to verify compliance before a project can receive its certificate of completion.

While Westgate is not classified as part of the High-Velocity Hurricane Zone (HVHZ) — a designation that applies to Miami-Dade County and portions of Broward County and triggers even more rigorous product requirements — the Florida Building Code still mandates that all hurricane-rated products installed in Palm Beach County carry an approved Notice of Acceptance (NOA) or equivalent product approval documentation. Each window and door product we install at Premier Impact Windows & Roofing comes with full NOA documentation, and our team prepares and submits all permit applications to the Palm Beach County Building Division on your behalf.

For Westgate's significant stock of pre-1994 homes, code compliance during a window replacement project also involves an energy efficiency component. Florida's Energy Conservation Code requires that replacement windows in new openings or significantly altered openings meet specific Solar Heat Gain Coefficient (SHGC) and U-factor ratings — both of which are addressed by the Low-E coated, laminated glass products we carry. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's expertise with Palm Beach County's permitting process ensures your project is completed correctly, inspected properly, and fully documented — which is critical when it comes time to file wind mitigation reports with your insurance carrier.

section_products.h2: ESW and CWI Impact Windows: Premium Products Built for Westgate's Climate
section_products.body:
Premier Impact Windows & Roofing installs two industry-leading product lines that are ideally suited to the climate conditions, code requirements, and home styles found throughout Westgate: ESW (Eastern Storm Windows) and CWI. Both brands are manufactured to meet or exceed Florida Building Code requirements for Palm Beach County's 140 mph wind zone, and both offer product lines specifically engineered for the South Florida environment — where salt air, intense UV radiation, high humidity, and hurricane-force wind events are all part of the long-term performance equation.

ESW's impact window systems are built around laminated safety glass — two panes of tempered or annealed glass bonded to a durable interlayer that holds the glass together even when struck by hurricane-driven debris. This means that even if the outer pane is cracked by an impact, the window maintains its structural integrity and continues to protect your home's interior from wind pressure and rain infiltration. ESW frames are constructed with multi-chamber PVC or aluminum profiles that provide exceptional rigidity while also delivering superior thermal insulation — a critical feature in Westgate's hot, humid summers. Low-E coating on the glass significantly reduces solar heat gain, helping to lower cooling costs in homes that can otherwise see elevated energy bills during the long South Florida summer.

CWI's product line offers comparable impact performance with a slightly different range of frame styles and configurations, making it an excellent choice for Westgate homeowners whose older homes may have architectural details or non-standard opening sizes that benefit from more customization flexibility. CWI windows are also engineered with multi-chamber frames and carry the same laminated glass technology, providing the dual certification of impact resistance and energy efficiency that Palm Beach County code requires. Together, ESW and CWI give our customers a range of choices in terms of aesthetics, frame color, glass tint, and grid patterns — so your new impact windows protect your home without compromising its character.

section_cost.h2: What Impact Windows Cost in Westgate — and How Premier Impact Windows & Roofing Makes Them Affordable
section_cost.body:
For a Westgate home with an average value of around $245,000, the cost of a full impact window replacement will vary depending on the number of openings, the size and configuration of each window, and the product line selected. In general, homeowners in this part of Palm Beach County can expect to invest in the range of $800 to $1,500 per window opening for a complete supply-and-install project using certified impact products like ESW or CWI. A typical Westgate home with 10 to 15 windows and one or two impact doors might represent a total investment of $10,000 to $20,000, though every home is different and our estimates are always free and tailored to your specific property.

We understand that for many families in Westgate, this is a significant financial decision — which is why Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers flexible financing options designed to make impact window upgrades accessible. Our financing programs start from as low as $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. When you factor in the energy savings from Low-E coated impact glass — which can meaningfully reduce monthly cooling costs in a South Florida home — and the insurance premium discounts that typically follow the installation of wind mitigation improvements, many Westgate homeowners find that their monthly financing payment is partially or fully offset by those savings from day one.

From a long-term investment perspective, impact windows consistently add measurable value to Palm Beach County homes. Real estate professionals across the region note that impact window upgrades are among the highest-ROI improvements a homeowner can make, both in terms of resale value and time-on-market performance. In Westgate, where older housing stock competes with newer construction in neighboring markets like West Palm Beach and Lake Clarke Shores, impact windows can meaningfully differentiate your home and justify a stronger asking price when you're ready to sell.

section_insurance.h2: Insurance Savings for Westgate Homeowners Who Upgrade to Impact Windows
section_insurance.body:
One of the most compelling financial benefits of installing impact windows in Westgate is the potential for significant homeowner's insurance premium reductions. Florida insurance carriers are required by state law to provide wind mitigation discounts when a home demonstrates qualifying protection features — and impact-rated windows on all openings, paired with a certified wind mitigation inspection, can produce some of the most substantial discounts available to Palm Beach County homeowners. Depending on your current carrier and policy structure, upgrading your entire home to impact windows and doors can result in wind premium reductions ranging from 20% to 45% or more.

Carriers active in the Palm Beach County market — including Citizens Property Insurance, Universal Property & Casualty, Heritage Insurance, and others — all recognize the protective value of impact-rated openings and factor them into their wind mitigation rating forms. The key document that unlocks these savings is the OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation Verification Form, which must be completed by a licensed inspector after your installation is complete. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing's projects are completed to full permit compliance, making the wind mitigation inspection process straightforward and ensuring your documentation is accurate and defensible with any carrier.

For Westgate homeowners who are currently paying elevated premiums for a home with original single-pane windows — particularly in the large pool of pre-1994 properties in the area — the combination of financing costs and insurance savings can dramatically change the financial calculus of this investment. Many customers in similar Palm Beach County communities find that their annual insurance premium reduction partially or fully offsets their monthly financing payment, effectively allowing them to upgrade their home's protection at little or no net monthly cost. We encourage every Westgate homeowner to request a wind mitigation estimate alongside their window quote so you can see the full financial picture before making a decision.

faqs.q: Do I really need impact windows in Westgate if I'm not right on the coast?
faqs.a: Absolutely. Westgate is located approximately 7 miles from the Atlantic coast, which means it falls within Palm Beach County's 140 mph wind zone and is subject to the same hurricane-force wind and debris events that coastal communities face. Hurricanes Frances, Wilma, and Irma all caused significant damage in this inland area. Distance from the water provides no meaningful wind protection during a major storm event, and Florida Building Code requires hurricane-rated products in all permitted window installations in the 33406 ZIP code.

faqs.q: How much do impact windows cost for a typical Westgate home, and what financing is available?
faqs.a: For a typical Westgate home with 10 to 15 windows, you can expect to invest in the range of $10,000 to $20,000 for a full supply-and-install project using ESW or CWI impact windows. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing offers financing starting from $87 per month with $0 down options available for qualified buyers. When you factor in energy savings and potential insurance premium reductions, your net monthly cost is often lower than the financing payment alone. We provide free, no-obligation in-home estimates tailored to your specific home.

faqs.q: What permits are required to replace windows in Westgate, and does Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handle that process?
faqs.a: Yes — all window replacements in Westgate require a building permit issued by the Palm Beach County Building Division. The process involves submitting product approvals (Notice of Acceptance documentation) along with installation drawings and owner authorization. Premier Impact Windows & Roofing handles the entire permit process on your behalf, from application through final inspection. This ensures your project is fully code-compliant, which is essential for insurance wind mitigation documentation and for protecting your home's resale value.

faqs.q: How long does a full impact window installation take for a Westgate home?
faqs.a: For a typical Westgate home with 10 to 15 windows, the physical installation is generally completed in one to two days by our in-house crew. Permit approval timelines through the Palm Beach County Building Division can vary but typically run one to three weeks depending on current volume. We coordinate the scheduling to minimize disruption to your household, and our team handles all cleanup and debris removal. From the time you sign your contract to final permit inspection, most projects are fully complete within three to six weeks.

faqs.q: How much can I save on homeowner's insurance after installing impact windows in Westgate?
faqs.a: Insurance savings vary by carrier and policy, but Palm Beach County homeowners who upgrade to impact windows on all openings — including doors — commonly see wind premium reductions of 20% to 45% or more after a certified wind mitigation inspection. To capture these savings, you'll need a completed OIR-B1-1802 Wind Mitigation Verification Form from a licensed inspector, which is straightforward to obtain once your permitted installation is finalized. We recommend contacting your insurance agent before installation to get a preliminary estimate of your expected savings based on your current policy.

cta_headline: Ready to Protect Your Westgate Home? Get Your Free Impact Window Estimate Today.
cta_body: Premier Impact Windows & Roofing — The Kings of Service — is ready to help Westgate homeowners secure their homes with the finest hurricane-rated impact windows available. Contact us today for a free, no-obligation in-home estimate and find out how affordable permanent storm protection can be.
